summary: Deprecated audit trail for the Offline Files binary-rename subrecord. The app renames mobsync.exe as part of its broader Offline Files shutdown bundle, but the publishable control surface is tracked separately in the validated Offline Files policy record.

setting.name: Offline Files Sync Center Binary Rename
setting.casual_explanation: This sub-tweak renames the Sync Center executable so the Offline Files user interface is harder to launch through that entry point.
setting.why_people_change_it: People do this when they want a hard shutdown of Sync Center behavior after already deciding to disable Offline Files.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Renaming the binary can reduce access to the Sync Center entry point, but it is much more invasive than using only the documented feature policy.
setting.professional_notes: The provider renames C:\Windows\System32\mobsync.exe to mobsync.exe.disabled. This is tracked as an observed implementation detail rather than an official Microsoft feature-control surface.

allowed_values.label: Default file state
allowed_values.meaning: The Sync Center executable is present at its normal path.

allowed_values.label: Renamed by app bundle
allowed_values.meaning: The current app renames the executable to a .disabled name.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app renames mobsync.exe to mobsync.exe.disabled as part of the Offline Files bundle.

windows_defaults.label: Normal binary state
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems that ship Sync Center

states.rationale: Treat the normal executable path as the baseline.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems using policy-only Offline Files management
avoid_for: Research comparison of the current app bundle

states.rationale: Keep the normal binary state unless there is a stronger reason to rename it.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the least invasive baseline.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are intentionally reproducing the product's broader shutdown bundle.
tradeoffs: Does not remove this Sync Center entry point.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app bundle

states.rationale: Track the file rename behavior the app currently applies.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting the most aggressive current implementation path.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want only the validated Offline Files policy control.
tradeoffs: Harder shutdown of a Sync Center entry point.
Much more invasive than the feature policy alone.

evidence.title: Current privacy provider file rename
evidence.summary: The provider renames mobsync.exe to mobsync.exe.disabled for the current sub-tweak.

decision.why: This is a strong implementation-level file rename and should not be treated as the same thing as the official feature policy.
blocking_issues: Binary rename goes beyond the validated Offline Files policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Current privacy provider file rename: The provider renames mobsync.exe to mobsync.exe.disabled for the current sub-tweak.
